reason: The remediation modifies the /boot entry in /etc/fstab, a core filesystem configuration. Misconfiguration can prevent the boot partition from mounting, leading to an unbootable system. Per policy, partitioning/filesystem changes are classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential boot failure; System may become unbootable if fstab syntax is incorrect; Loss of console or remote access until recovery
